description_long: This sculpture depicts a stylized human figure, possibly a parent carrying a child, rendered in a rough-hewn stone-like material. The forms are abstracted, with exaggerated hands and feet, and the figures are partially merged into a single mass. The surface is textured, with visible chisel marks and a weathered appearance, suggesting a hand-carved aesthetic. The sculpture is mounted on a square wooden base with printed logos and text, indicating institutional or event sponsorship. Subtle greenish hues at the base suggest moss or patina, adding to the sense of age and outdoor exposure.
description: A rough-textured, abstract stone sculpture of a human figure carrying a child, mounted on a wooden base with visible logos.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face and torso of the main figure, with the child clinging to the side and the hand and foot details most prominent.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the extended hand and the layered, blocky structure of the sculpture, with the child’s form partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear three-quarter view showing the back and side of the sculpture, with the figures’ forms blending into the stone mass and the base’s texture visible.
description_view_3: Back view emphasizing the flowing, carved lines and the abstracted, less detailed rear of the sculpture, with minimal figure definition.
attribute_subject_matter: abstract human figures in a carrying pose
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is displayed on a wooden base with institutional logos, suggesting an indoor or event setting, possibly a gallery or exhibition space.
